persona_prompt:
You are filtering Boston, Cambridge, and Providence RI events for Kirk Brown.

Kirk's background:
- Senior Sales Engineer — sells and demos complex technical products to technical buyers
- SE at Mapbox (SoftBank-backed geo/spatial tech) and Bit Flow (machine vision / industrial hardware)
- Deep hands-on expertise: hardware engineering, software engineering, and technical sales
- Finishing his master's degree
- Domain expertise: geo/spatial tech, machine vision, industrial automation, semiconductors, data infrastructure

Kirk needs events where a senior technical sales person is VALUED — not events aimed at founders pitching VCs, and not job fairs that attract mostly junior candidates. The ideal room has:
- VCs, CTOs, and technical founders who need someone like Kirk to help sell or evaluate products
- Senior operators at startups (not entry-level), people building and shipping real products
- Events where deep product knowledge and demo skills matter

What Kirk wants:
- Deep tech product launches and demos: hardware, robotics, embedded systems, IoT, machine vision, industrial tech, semiconductors — events where the product is the star and technical sales people belong
- GTM & revenue leadership: CRO panels, VP Sales roundtables, technical sales strategy, B2B go-to-market
- Sales Engineer / presales community: SE community events, Pavilion SE chapter, SE Collective, PreSales Collective
- Defense & deep tech: defense tech startups, dual-use hardware, MIT Lincoln Lab adjacent, Draper Lab, MITRE
- VC-hosted portfolio events: ecosystem mixers (not pitch competitions)
- Partner ecosystem events: Salesforce/HubSpot ecosystem events where SEs network with other SEs
- University research-to-industry: MIT, Harvard, Brown, Northeastern, WPI — hardware/robotics/EE events
- Geographic range: Boston, Cambridge, Somerville, Providence RI — has a car, will travel for quality

SCORE 9-10 (must attend):
- Hardware/deep tech product demo events (Greentown Labs showcases, MassRobotics demos, MIT hardware)
- Semiconductor or chip design events with industry networking (ARM, Analog Devices, MIT MTL, IEEE Boston)
- Sales Engineer or presales community events (Pavilion SE chapter, SE Collective, PreSales Collective)
- Defense tech or dual-use hardware events open to industry (Draper, MITRE, DIU)
- GTM/revenue leadership panels with VP+ speakers — technical sales focus
- VC portfolio company mixers or deep tech ecosystem events (not pitch competitions)
- Robotics showcases with real industry participation (MassRobotics, Boston Dynamics adjacent)

SCORE 5-7 (worth knowing):
- Senior tech networking with solid company mix — startups with real products, not just ideas
- University EE/CS/robotics showcases with industry crossover
- CTO or engineering leadership panels at companies in Kirk's domains
- SaaS or hardware product launches in relevant sectors
- Maker/hardware community events (Artisan's Asylum) if the crowd skews professional

LOWER PRIORITY (score ≤ 4):
- Pitch nights and demo days aimed at founders pitching investors (Kirk isn't pitching)
- Job fairs, career fairs, or 'hiring mixer' events (attract too many junior candidates)
- Generic startup networking or 'founder meetups' with no technical depth
- Events clearly targeted at undergrads or early-career with no senior presence
- Pure academic lectures with no networking or industry angle
- Sports, arts-only, food/drink without tech angle
- Basic networking mixers with no engineering or sales focus

events.name: BU AI Research Updates and AI-Powered Pathology
events.description:
Let's get together, eat pizza, and hear from academic AI researchers, local companies and experts in the field. This week we will get **short updates on cutting-edge computer vision and AI research from Boston University PhD students** and hear from **Harsha Vardhan Pokkalla ([LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/harshapokkalla/)),**
**VP of ML R&D at PathAI**.

PathAI builds best-in-class AI-powered pathology products for biopharma and diagnostic labs, aiming to discover histology biomarkers a
events.venue: BU Faculty of Computing & Data Sciences (CDS)
events.reason: BU AI Research + PathAI VP of ML R&D. Strong technical content (computer vision, AI research), company expertise in regulated industry (pathology/biopharma). Mix of academic and applied industry perspectives. Real technical credibility.

events.name: ClawCon Boston
events.reason: ClawCon — likely robotics/automation focus (claw-based systems); strong fit for Kirk's industrial automation and robotics interests

events.name: Ramp @ Cambridge: What It Means to Build Now
events.reason: MIT-hosted 'Build Now' event — likely hardware/deep tech focus with strong technical founder and operator presence; high-quality Boston-area ecosystem

events.name: Training Day 2: AI networking on K8s
events.description:
**Training Day 2: AI networking on K8s**

Last year (Sept 2025) was a big success. This time the content is more advanced: AI networking on K8s. Again this will include:

* **Awesome content**: Kubernetes training with HANDS ON labs
* **Instructor-led**: Michael O'Leary and Benjamin Hautefeuille
* **Amazing location**: Microsoft NERD Center, Cambridge
* **Food/bev:** Breakfast, lunch & snacks provided
* **Cost:** $40 pp ($25 if you register early)
* **Limit**: 60 people
* **Date**: Saturday, May
events.venue: Microsoft New England Research & Development Center (NERD)
events.reason: Kubernetes and AI networking hands-on training at Microsoft NERD Center. Technical depth, instructor-led, limited size (60 people). Relevant to infrastructure and modern deployment practices Kirk encounters in sales engineer conversations.

events.name: Scale Enterprise AI Agents
events.reason: Enterprise AI Agents — infrastructure and deployment angle, relevant to data/AI sales engineering and GTM in enterprise tech

events.name: GitHub Copilot Dev Days - Boston
events.description:
Register on [AICamp event website](https://www.aicamp.ai/event/eventdetails/W2026042913) is REQUIRED for admission.

**Description:**
Join us for a community-led developer event focused on AI-assisted coding with GitHub Copilot. This event brings together developers to explore practical workflows, real-world use cases, and hands-on experiences using GitHub Copilot.
Whether you are new to GitHub Copilot or already using it, this event will help you better understand how to apply AI-assisted codin
events.venue: Microsoft New England Research and Development Center
events.reason: Developer-focused AI coding event. Peripherally relevant to Kirk's technical background, but primarily aimed at software engineers rather than technical sales professionals or hardware/industrial tech buyers. Lower priority.

events.name: Zerve × ODSC AI: Datathon | Boston/Virtual
events.reason: Datathon with ODSC — data infrastructure, technical competition, startup-friendly ecosystem; potential for quality technical networking

events.name: Fireside w/ Bumble and Slack CEO Lidiane Jones & Ando CEO Sara Du
events.reason: Slack CEO fireside — enterprise SaaS leader and ecosystem perspective; worth attending for GTM insights despite not hardware-specific

restaurants.name: Zuma
restaurants.editorial: High-energy Japanese izakaya in the Seaport. Known for a lively bar scene, beautiful room, and a crowd that dresses up. Popular for late dinner and drinks.

restaurants.name: Desnuda
restaurants.editorial: Intimate raw bar and natural wine spot. Industry crowd, low-key cool. East Boston location and Seaport outpost.
